heading: Optional use of a one-step summoning and qualification procedure
label: 1878 Optional use of a one-step summoning and qualification procedure
notes: Amendments1992— substituted “Optional” for “Experimental” in section catchline and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows:Pub. L. 102–572“(a) The Judicial Conference of the United States is hereby authorized to develop and conduct an experiment in which jurors serving in a limited number of United States district courts shall be qualified and summoned in a single procedure, in lieu of the two separate procedures otherwise provided for by this chapter. The Judicial Conference shall designate the district courts to participate in this experiment, but in no event shall the number of courts participating exceed ten. An experiment may be conducted pursuant to this section for a period not to exceed 2 years. The Judicial Conference shall ensure that an experiment conducted pursuant to this section does not violate the policies and objectives set forth in sections 1861 and 1862 of this title, and shall terminate the experiment immediately if it determines that these policies and objectives are being violated or whenever in its judgment good cause for such termination exists.“(b) Jury selection conducted pursuant to this section shall be subject to challenge under for substantial failure to comply with the provisions of this title in selecting the jury. However, no challenge under shall lie solely on the basis that a jury was selected in accordance with an experiment conducted pursuant to this section.”section 1867 of this titlesection 1867 of this title
Effective Date of 1992 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under , The Congress.Pub. L. 102–572section 1101(a) of Pub. L. 102–572section 905 of Title 2Jan. 1, 1993

children.label: a
children.text: At the option of each district court, jurors may be summoned and qualified in a single procedure, if the court’s jury selection plan so authorizes, in lieu of the two separate procedures otherwise provided for by this chapter. Courts shall ensure that a one-step summoning and qualification procedure conducted under this section does not violate the policies and objectives set forth in sections 1861 and 1862 of this title.

children.label: b
children.text: Jury selection conducted under this section shall be subject to challenge under for substantial failure to comply with the provisions of this title in selecting the jury. However, no challenge under shall lie solely on the basis that a jury was selected in accordance with a one-step summoning and qualification procedure authorized by this section.section 1867 of this titlesection 1867 of this title
